---
title: "Week 10 Milestone Params"
subtitle: "`r params$year` Report for `r params$herd`" 
author: "<img src='images/logo.png' width='50' /> <br> Dairy Cow Foot Doc"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
format:
  html:
    page-layout: full
    theme: flatly
    df_print: paged
    toc: true
    toc-location: left
    number-sections: false
    code-fold: true
execute:
  message: false
  warning: false
  echo: false
embed-resources: true
params:
  herd: "Herd 2108e"
  year: 2024
---

```{r}
#| label: setup
#| include: false

if (!require("pacman")) install.packages("pacman")
pacman::p_load(
  broom,
  tidyverse,
  dtplyr,
  gt,
  arrow,
  rmarkdown,
  lubridate,
  quarto, 
  arrow,
  survival,
  survminer,
  ggsurvfit,
  flextable,
  gtsummary,
  marginaleffects,
  scales,
  zoo
  )

year_filter <- make_date(params$year, 1, 1)

events_formatted <- read_parquet(
  '../data/intermediate_files/events_formatted.parquet')|> 
  filter(location_event %in% params$herd) |> 
  filter(date_event >= year_filter & date_event < year_filter + years(1))

animals <- read_parquet(
  '../data/intermediate_files/animals.parquet') |> 
  filter(location_list %in% params$herd) 

deno_cal <- read_parquet(
  '../data/intermediate_files/denominator_by_calendar_time_period.parquet') |> 
  filter(deno_type %in% c("lact_basic", "lact_5+") & 
           calendar_time_period_type == "year") |> 
  select(-c(contains("dop"), day_of_phase_group)) |> 
  filter(location_lact_list %in% params$herd) |> 
  filter(date_time_period_start >= year_filter & date_time_period_start < year_filter + years(1))

  
# load GC's functions
source(here::here('functions/fxn_load_os_fxns.R'))
fxn_load_os_fxns()

# Load Parnell CR fucntions from Github
cr_url <- "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ParnellPharma/ParnellFunctionsPublic/main/parnell_calculate_CR_master.R"
source(cr_url)

herd <- params$herd

farm_name <- unique(events_formatted$location_event)

# create variables for inline code

date_max_pull <- max(events_formatted$date_event, na.rm = TRUE)
date_min_pull <- date_max_pull - years(1)

# pull data for graphs
source(here::here('functions/fxn_milestone8_solution.R'))


```

```{r}
#| label: functions to hide my code
#| echo: false

solutions_deno <- function () {
  
deno_table <- deno_cal |> 
  select(location_lact_list, 
         date_time_period_start,
         group = 'Lactation Group' , ct_animal_days_elig, 
         ct_animal_time_periods, ct_animal_lactations,
         ct_animals) |>
  mutate(group = if_else(group == "LACT > 0",
                                    "Adult Cows", group)
         ) |>
  gt(groupname_col = "location_lact_list",
     rowname_col = "date_time_period_start") |> 
  fmt_date(columns = date_time_period_start,
           date_style = "year") |> 
  fmt_number(columns = c(starts_with("ct")), decimals = 0) |> 
  tab_style(
    style = cell_text(weight = "bold"),
    locations = cells_column_labels()
  ) |>
    cols_label(
      group = "Group",
    date_time_period_start = "Time Period",
    ct_animal_days_elig = "Days at risk",
    ct_animal_time_periods = "DAR Animals",
    ct_animal_lactations = "Lactations",
    ct_animals = "Animals"
    ) |> 
  tab_spanner(
    label = "Count of",
    columns = c(ct_animal_days_elig, ct_animal_time_periods,
                ct_animal_lactations, ct_animals)
  ) |>
    tab_style(
    style = cell_text(weight = "bold"),
    locations = cells_column_spanners()
  ) |> 
    tab_style(
    style = cell_text(weight = "bold"),
    locations = cells_column_labels()
  ) |> 
  tab_options(
    row_group.font.weight = "bold" # Bolds the Location  
  ) 
    
    
deno_table

}

solutions_deno_lact <- function () {
  
deno_table <- deno_cal |> 
  select(location_lact_list, 
         date_time_period_start,
         group = 'Lactation Group' , ct_animal_days_elig, 
         ct_animal_time_periods, ct_animal_lactations,
         ct_animals) |>
  filter(!group %in% c("Heifer","LACT > 0" )) |>
  gt(groupname_col = "location_lact_list",
     rowname_col = "date_time_period_start") |> 
  fmt_date(columns = date_time_period_start,
           date_style = "year") |> 
  fmt_number(columns = c(starts_with("ct")), decimals = 0) |> 
  tab_style(
    style = cell_text(weight = "bold"),
    locations = cells_column_labels()
  ) |>
    cols_label(
      group = "Group",
    date_time_period_start = "Time Period",
    ct_animal_days_elig = "Days at risk",
    ct_animal_time_periods = "DAR Animals",
    ct_animal_lactations = "Lactations",
    ct_animals = "Animals"
    ) |> 
  tab_spanner(
    label = "Count of",
    columns = c(ct_animal_days_elig, ct_animal_time_periods,
                ct_animal_lactations, ct_animals)
  ) |>
    tab_style(
    style = cell_text(weight = "bold"),
    locations = cells_column_spanners()
  ) |> 
    tab_style(
    style = cell_text(weight = "bold"),
    locations = cells_column_labels()
  ) |> 
  tab_options(
    row_group.font.weight = "bold" # Bolds the Location  
  ) 
    
    
deno_table

}


# CR

solutions_cr_data <- function () {
  
cr_data <- events_formatted |> 
  select(id_animal, date_birth, date_fresh,lact_number, lact_group_5, 
         date_archived, date_event, event, 
         remark_letters1, protocols_letters1, location_event, R) |> 
  mutate(floor_date_time = floor_date(date_event, "year")) |>
  filter(date_event <= date_max_pull - days(40)) |>
  fxn_create_CR_reports(grp_vars = c("location_event", 
                                     "floor_date_time", 
                                     "lact_group_5", "Rstandard"))

return(cr_data)
}

solutions_cr_table <- function() {
cr_table <- cr_data |> 
  select(location_event, floor_date_time,
         lact_group_5, `Not Bred Event`,
         Preg, Total, SPC, CR, CI_lower, CI_upper) |> 
  mutate(CR = CR*100,
         CR_graph = CR,
        ) |> 
  arrange(location_event, floor_date_time, lact_group_5) |>
  # 1. Group by location_event to "merge" that column into group headers
  gt(groupname_col = "location_event",
     rowname_col = "floor_date_time") |> 
  # 2. Bold the column labels
  tab_style(
    style = cell_text(weight = "bold"),
    locations = cells_column_labels()
  ) |> 
  # Optional: Clean up the date display
  fmt_date(columns = floor_date_time, date_style = "year") |> 
  fmt_number(c(CR, CI_lower, CI_upper, SPC), 
             decimals = 1) |> 
  fmt_number(columns = `Not Bred Event`, decimals = 0) |> 
  tab_options(
    row_group.font.weight = "bold" # Bolds the Location  
  )  |> 
  cols_merge_range(col_begin = CI_lower,
                  col_end = CI_upper) |> 
  cols_label(
    floor_date_time = "Time Period",
    lact_group_5 = "Lactation",
    CR = "Risk",
    CI_lower = "95% CI",
    ) |>
  cols_nanoplot(columns = c(CR_graph),
                plot_type = "bar",
                new_col_name = "Plot",
                new_col_label = md("**Plot**"),
                options = nanoplot_options(data_bar_fill_color = "orange",
                                           data_bar_stroke_color = "black")
    ) |> 
  summary_rows(
    groups = everything(), # Adds summary to every group
    columns = c(CR), # Columns you want to summarize
    fns = list(
      "Location Average" = ~mean(., na.rm = TRUE)
    ),
    fmt = ~fmt_number(., columns = c(CR), decimals = 1)
    )|> 
  tab_spanner(
    label = "Conception (%)",
    columns = c(CR, CI_lower, 'Plot')
  ) |>
    tab_style(
    style = cell_text(weight = "bold"),
    locations = cells_column_spanners()
  ) |> 
  cols_align(align = "center",
             columns = everything()) |> 
  tab_style(locations = cells_summary(),
             style = list(
               cell_text(weight = "bold"),
               cell_fill("lightblue")
             )
            ) 
 cr_table
}

solutions_violin <- function () {
  
violin <- log_data |> 
  ggplot(aes(x = days_to_cull, y = fct_rev(lesion),
             fill = lesion)
                  ) +
  geom_violin(draw_quantiles = c(0.25, 0.5, 0.75),
              quantile.colour = "red",
              scale = "count")+
  theme_minimal()+
  labs(x = "Days to Culling after Trim",
       y = "")+
  scale_fill_viridis_d()+
  theme(legend.position = "none")


violin

}

# heat map of culled 30 with denominator # cows trimmed

solutions_heatmap <- function () {
heat_map_lact <- log_data |>
  group_by(noninf, lact_group, culled_30) |> 
  summarise(count = n(), .groups = "drop") |> 
  group_by(noninf, lact_group) |>
  mutate(pct = count / sum(count)) |> 
  ungroup()

heat_map_sum <- log_data |> 
  group_by(noninf, culled_30) |>
  summarise(count = n(), .groups = "drop") |>
  group_by(noninf) |>
  mutate(pct = count / sum(count),
         lact_group = "Overall") |> 
  ungroup()


heat_map <- bind_rows(heat_map_lact, heat_map_sum)
 

heat_plot <- heat_map |> 
  filter(culled_30 == 1) |>
  mutate(lact_group = fct_relevel(lact_group, 
                                  c("LACT 3+", 
                                    "LACT 2", 
                                    "LACT 1", 
                                    "Overall"))                                    ) |> 
  ggplot(aes(x = noninf, y = fct_rev(lact_group),
             fill = pct)
         ) +
  geom_tile()+
  geom_text(aes(label = str_glue(
    "{round(pct * 100, 1)}% ({count})")),
          color = "black", 
          size = 3)+
  scale_fill_viridis_c(name = "% Culled within 30 days",
                       labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 1),
                       alpha = 0.8,
                       option = "C")+
  theme_minimal()+
  labs(x = "",
      y = "")+
  theme(axis.ticks.y=element_blank())

heat_plot
}

```

```{r}
#| label: create-inline-variables
#| echo: false

max_cr <- solutions_cr_data()|> 
  filter(lact_group_5 != "Heifer") |> 
  mutate(lact_group_5 = str_replace(lact_group_5, "LACT", "Lactation")) |> 
  slice_max(CR) |> 
  select(lact_group_5, CR) |> 
  mutate(CR = round(CR*100, digits = 1))

heifer_cr <- solutions_cr_data()|> 
  filter(lact_group_5 == "Heifer") |> 
  mutate(CR = round(CR*100, digits = 1)) |> 
  pull(CR)

compare <- ifelse(heifer_cr > max_cr$CR, "higher", "lower")

cows <- deno_cal |> 
  filter(`Lactation Group` == "LACT > 0") |> 
  pull(ct_animals)


```

# Report for `r herd`

## Objective

The objective of this milestone is to run a report over multiple herds
and years. Feel free to use existing code from previous milestones to
minimize the time spend creating new code. Try to include some in line R
code in the text that changes based on the herd you are working with.

The steps for this milestone are as follows (code sections for these
steps are below)

1.  Re-Run **step0_master_processing.R** if you did not do it last week
    to give you at least 2 herds to run a report on.

2.  I did not load any github functions automatically so if you want to
    call the functions we used previously you will need to load these.

3.  Set *params* in the yaml to include herd and year.

4.  Insert some tables and graphs from previous milestones into this
    document using tabs/columns or whatever layout you prefer. The data
    in these tables/graphs should be for each herd and for each year.

5.  Once you have a completed report for one herd and year you can use
    quarto to render reports for multiple herds/years by using the file
    ***milestone_10_render.R*** in the main directory (same one as step 0)  

    -   If you are using your own data adjust the herd names and years
        in the code of that document.
        -  You can also just run the report for 1 year by setting params to 1 year.

# Conception Risk Comparison

This herd of **`r cows`** cows had a conception risk of
**`r heifer_cr`%** in heifers of in **`r params$year`**. Compared to
lactating cows this heifer CR was **`r compare`** than the highest
conception risk **(`r max_cr$CR`%)** in **`r max_cr$lact_group_5`**

# Tables

:::: panel-tabset
## Denominators

::: panel-tabset
### Herd Size

```{r}
#| label: describe-herds

solutions_deno()


```

### Lactation \#'s

```{r}


solutions_deno_lact()
```
:::

## Conception Risk

```{r}
#| label: create-cr_table

cr_data <- solutions_cr_data() 
solutions_cr_table()

```
::::

# Graphs

::: panel-tabset
## Lameness timing

```{r}
#| label: create-violin
#| echo: true

solutions_violin()

```

## Lesions Risk

```{r}
#| label: create-heatmap
#| echo: true  

solutions_heatmap()

```
:::

# Extension

For this week extension continue to create standard things you would
want to report or incorporate modeling techniques to explore the data

Ideas

-   Change the params to change the report by something other than herd or year.
-   Finalize your report that you would use on a routine basis in your practice.
